title: grants — cross-module relations
description: AccessGrant is the aggregate root. It is polymorphic on TWO axes: (subject_type, subject_id) — the principal receiving the permission (today always User); (resource_type, resource_id) — the target the permission applies to (any domain aggregate that composes IsGrantable + registers via GrantableRegistry). Concrete tenant + granted_by / revoked_by FKs are hard; every other resource / subject reference is polymorphic. Cross-tenant FKs are forbidden — the observer refuses `resource.tenant_id !== subject.tenant_id !== grant.tenant_id` with GRANT_CROSS_TENANT.

relations.name: tenant
relations.description: The owning tenant. Applied via the BelongsToTenant trait — every read is auto-filtered on the tenant global scope.

relations.name: grantor
relations.description: The User who issued the grant. Nullable because system-issued grants (source in { 'access_request' where auto-approved, 'bulk_provision' }) may have no direct human grantor — provenance lives on the source_reference row instead.

relations.name: revoker
relations.description: The User who revoked the grant. NULL for system-driven revocations (PurgeExpiredGrantsJob, ReconcileOrphanedGrantsJob).

morphs.name: subject
morphs.description: The principal receiving the permission. Concrete class resolved by GrantableRegistry at hydrate time.

expectedSubjects.note: The only subject_type shipped in Wave 1b.
Reserved for Wave 2. resolver registration in place but no consumer registers `Group` as a target subject_type yet.

morphs.name: resource
morphs.description: The target resource the permission applies to. Concrete class resolved by GrantableRegistry at hydrate time. Consumer modules register `resource_type` keys + their class + optional preflight validator in their service provider boot; adding a grantable resource is one class + one register() call.

expectedResources.note: Grant edit / view access to a specific athlete's profile + progress data.
Grant coach-of-team access without a formal team_members row.
Grant view / edit / refund access on a specific invoice (the canonical Google-Docs-style share flow).
Grant read / comment / edit on tenant-authored documents (attendance reports, coach evaluations, meeting notes).

outbound.description: Grants module reads FROM these tables but does not own the FK constraint (polymorphic morph OR read-only cross-reference).

reads.validated_by: PermissionCatalogueValidator (write-time check against access/rbac's permissions table; unknown names refused with PERMISSION_UNKNOWN)
reads.description: Every string in the permissions JSONB array must resolve to a live permission row. Not a hard FK because the array is JSONB — the validator enforces referential integrity at write time.
Not FK-constrained (polymorphic across sources). When source='access_request', source_reference matches an approval_instance.id (`api_<26>`) — used for reverse-lookup + audit reconciliation.
Reserved for the delegation-driven flow. Matches role_delegations.id (`dlg_<26>`) when a delegator grants access on a resource to their delegate.

inbound.description: Models composing HasResourceGrants expose a MorphMany relation TO AccessGrant. This module does not track inbound consumers directly — GrantableRegistry is the canonical registry.
consumers_at_wave_1b: teams::Team → grants for coach-of-team without team_members row

notes: Polymorphic subject + resource are the extension seams. The grants module does NOT know that `athlete` means Sports' Athlete — it just knows a string key + registration hooked at boot.

GrantableRegistry is a container binding published by this module. Downstream modules register their resource_type + optional preflight validator + display-label at boot. Zero schema changes when a new domain aggregate becomes grantable.
Tenant + granted_by + revoked_by are hard FKs — polymorphism ends at the tenancy + user substrates. Every grant lives strictly inside its tenant; every grantor / revoker is a real User.

AccessGrant does NOT compose BelongsToApplication. application_id cascades through tenant_id — grants are NOT one of the eight rows in `.kiro/steering/tenancy-columns.md §2` that carry application_id directly.
